Stats: 51 likes, 0 retweets, 0 quote tweets, 2 replies Sam Trabucco (@AlamedaTrabucco) Tweet ID: 1451281811586359301 Date: 2021-10-21 20:18:42 GMT Replying to: @AlamedaTrabucco (Tweet ID: 1451281810424467456) Body: CON: getting rekt The price to pay for increased position sizing is that the exchange needs to liquidate you *before* you go bankrupt, which means that there's asymmetry introduced and people end up going bankrupt more often. Stats: 49 likes, 0 retweets, 0 quote tweets, 4 replies Sam Trabucco (@AlamedaTrabucco) Tweet ID: 1451281810424467456 Date: 2021-10-21 20:18:41 GMT Replying to: @AlamedaTrabucco (Tweet ID: 1451281809325629443) Body: PRO: scaling up If a great trader is suddenly allowed to put their trades on 2x as big, that's great for them! And really good risk managers can often afford to lever up even more, which is especially nice in a world with so many uses for capital (trading, yield, NFTs, etc.).
